title: Tanis (San El-Hagar) Archaeological Site
era: Third Intermediate Period (21st & 22nd Dynasties) to Ptolemaic
description: The 'Thebes of the North', serving as the capital of Egypt during the Third Intermediate Period. It contains a massive temple enclosure and royal necropolis.

title: Temple of Amun (Tanis)
description: The Great Temple dedicated to Amun-Ra, built using reused blocks and statues from Pi-Ramesses, featuring massive pylons and column bases.

title: Royal Tomb of Psusennes I
description: Discovered intact in 1940, this tomb belonged to the 'Silver King' and contained a magnificent silver coffin and golden mask.

title: Royal Tomb of Sheshonq III
description: The burial place of King Sheshonq III, part of the royal necropolis within the temple precinct.

title: Royal Tomb of Osorkon II
description: A large tomb constructed for Osorkon II, containing a massive granite sarcophagus.

title: Sacred Lake of Tanis
description: A well-preserved rectangular sacred lake used for ritual purity by the priests of Amun.

title: Obelisks Field
description: An area within the temple containing numerous broken obelisks of Ramesses II, transported from Pi-Ramesses.

title: Temple of Mut & Khonsu
description: Smaller temple structures dedicated to the other members of the Theban Triad.

title: Tell Basta (Bubastis) Archaeological Site
description: The ancient city of Bubastis, center of the worship of the cat goddess Bastet and capital of the 18th Lower Egyptian nome.

title: Temple of Bastet
description: The red granite temple of the goddess Bastet, described by Herodotus. Now largely a field of ruins and blocks.

title: Temple of Pepi I / Teti
description: Vestiges of a Ka-chapel or temple dating back to the Old Kingdom monarchs Pepi I and Teti.

title: Cat Cemetery
description: A series of mudbrick vaulted galleries where thousands of mummified cats were buried as votive offerings.

title: Holy Family Well
description: An ancient well believed to have been blessed by Jesus during the Holy Family's stay in Bubastis.

title: Colossal Statue of Meritamun
description: A magnificent colossal statue of Queen Meritamun, daughter/wife of Ramesses II, re-erected at the site.

title: Tell El-Daba'a (Avaris)
description: The site of the Hyksos capital Avaris. It revealed Minoan-style frescoes and a Thutmosid palace.

title: Pi-Ramesses (Qantir)
description: The Delta capital of Ramesses II. Excavations have revealed a horse stable, weapons factories, and statue bases.

title: Tell El-Shuqafiya
description: Archaeological mound with visible remains of mudbrick structures and a Roman settlement.

title: Saft El-Hinna Archaeological Site
description: Ancient Per-Sopdu, capital of the 20th Lower Egyptian nome. Known for the black granite naos found there.

title: Tell Phacusa (Faqous)
description: The ancient city of Phacusa, capital of the Arabian nome during the Roman period.

title: Tell El-Rataba
description: A major fortress guarding the approach to Egypt. Features walls of Ramesses II and Ramesses III.

title: Tell Iswid
description: An important settlement site dating back to the formation of the Egyptian state (Naqada culture in Delta).

title: Tell Ibrahim Awad
description: Site containing a sequence of shrines and tombs, crucial for understanding early religion in the Delta.

title: Gezira Sangaha
description: Archaeological site in the northern part of the governorate.

title: Tell Nabasha (Tell Farun)
description: Ancient Imet. Contains remains of a temple to Wadjet, a granite sarcophagus, and colossal statues.

title: Mosque of Sadat Quraish
description: Considered possibly the oldest mosque in Egypt (older than Amr Ibn Al-Aas in foundation legend), renovated over centuries.

title: Ahmed Orabi's House Museum
description: The birthplace of the nationalist leader Ahmed Orabi, now a museum dedicated to his life and the 1882 revolution.

title: King Farouk Rest House
description: A royal retreat belonging to King Farouk in the Inshas area.

title: Tell El-Qirqfa
description: Settlement site revealing domestic architecture from the Second Intermediate Period.

title: Tell Abu Yasin
description: Archaeological mound near Abu Kebir with extensive Roman remains.

title: Tell Umm Hagar
description: Site containing granaries and domestic structures.

title: Zagazig University Museum
description: University museum housing artifacts excavated by the university missions in Tell Basta and nearby tells.

title: Umm Kulthum Statue
description: Monument dedicated to the 'Star of the East', who was born in the governorate.

title: Talami Island
description: One of the archaeological islands in the lake, similar to Tinnis.

title: Tell Belim
description: Archaeological site with remains of a temple enclosure.

title: Tell El-Daba'a (Ezbet Rushdi)
description: Part of the greater Avaris complex, specifically the Middle Kingdom settlement of Amenemhat I.
